title: Interactive ARIA Fixes
description: Fix WCAG violations in interactive components: correct role='row' without grid parent in ApplicationRow, add unique aria-labels to SavedSearchesSidebar delete buttons, fix DigestSection label wrapping a switch button, add aria-modal to JobDetailPanel, fix KanbanCard stale dot aria usage, add aria-hidden to decorative SourceIcon in ApplicationRow, add aria-haspopup to NotificationBell trigger, and add role/label to CommandPalette results container.

userStories.title: Fix ApplicationRow role='row' WCAG violation
userStories.description: ApplicationRow.jsx renders its root div with role='row', but there is no parent element with role='grid' or role='table'. This is a WCAG failure — row roles must be owned by a grid, table, or rowgroup. Remove role='row' from the div and replace it with a semantically appropriate element or role that doesn't require a parent grid context.
acceptanceCriteria: ApplicationRow.jsx root element does not use role='row' without a parent grid/table context
The component renders as a div or li (not role='row') that is valid without a grid parent
All existing layout and styling classes are preserved
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add unique aria-labels to SavedSearchesSidebar delete buttons
userStories.description: SavedSearchesSidebar.jsx renders a delete button for each saved search, but all delete buttons share the same aria-label='Delete saved search'. Screen reader users cannot distinguish which search they are about to delete. Update each delete button's aria-label to include the search name, e.g. aria-label={`Delete saved search: ${search.name}`}.
acceptanceCriteria: Each delete button in SavedSearchesSidebar.jsx has a unique aria-label that includes the search name
The aria-label format is 'Delete saved search: {name}' or equivalent descriptive pattern
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Fix DigestSection label wrapping switch button double-announcement
userStories.description: DigestSection.jsx wraps a <button role='switch'> inside a <label> element. This causes screen readers to double-announce the control (once as label, once as button). Replace the <label> wrapper with a <div> and keep the text as a separate <span> so the label text and switch remain siblings rather than parent/child.
acceptanceCriteria: DigestSection.jsx outer wrapper is a <div> (or other non-label element), not a <label>

The descriptive text ('Weekly digest emails' or similar) is a sibling or

, not a label for the switch
The switch button still has an accessible name (either via aria-label or aria-labelledby pointing to the text span)
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add aria-modal='true' to JobDetailPanel dialog
userStories.description: JobDetailPanel.jsx has role='dialog' but is missing aria-modal='true'. Without aria-modal, screen readers may continue reading content behind the dialog. Add aria-modal='true' to the dialog root element.
acceptanceCriteria: JobDetailPanel.jsx dialog root element has aria-modal='true'
The element already has role='dialog' — both attributes should be present together
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Fix KanbanCard stale dot aria-label on non-interactive span
userStories.description: KanbanCard.jsx renders a stale indicator <span> with aria-label='Stale application…' nested inside a draggable/clickable card. The aria-label on a non-interactive span inside an interactive ancestor causes confusing announcements. Replace with aria-hidden='true' on the indicator span and add a <span className='sr-only'> with the stale message as a sibling.
acceptanceCriteria: KanbanCard.jsx stale indicator span has aria-hidden='true' instead of aria-label
A <span className='sr-only'> with the stale message text is rendered adjacent to (not inside) the indicator dot
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add aria-hidden to decorative SourceIcon in ApplicationRow
userStories.description: ApplicationRow.jsx renders a SourceIcon with aria-label={application.source}. Since the source name is already displayed as visible text nearby, the icon is decorative and its aria-label causes a duplicate announcement. Change the icon to aria-hidden='true' and remove the aria-label.
acceptanceCriteria: ApplicationRow.jsx SourceIcon has aria-hidden='true'
The aria-label on SourceIcon is removed
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add aria-haspopup to NotificationBell trigger button
userStories.description: NotificationBell.jsx trigger button has aria-expanded but is missing aria-haspopup='true' (or aria-haspopup='dialog'). Without aria-haspopup, screen readers don't announce that activating the button opens a popup. Add aria-haspopup='true' to the trigger button.
acceptanceCriteria: NotificationBell.jsx trigger button has aria-haspopup='true'
The button already has aria-expanded — both attributes should be present
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add role and aria-label to CommandPalette results container
userStories.description: CommandPalette.jsx renders search results in a scrollable div with no role or label. Screen reader users cannot identify the results region. Add role='listbox' and aria-label='Search results' (or role='list' with aria-label) to the results container div.
acceptanceCriteria: CommandPalette.jsx results container div has role='listbox' (or role='list') and aria-label='Search results'
The role is appropriate for the children (option items if listbox, listitem if list)
Build passes with no errors
